proposal_text: This research proposes to deploy Amazon Rekognition, a cloud-based facial recognition system, for use by law enforcement agencies across the United States to identify individuals in real-time surveillance footage, body camera recordings, and crime scene images. The system can identify and track up to 100 faces in a single image and match them against databases of tens of millions of faces drawn from arrest records and public sources. The system will be marketed to local police departments as a tool for identifying persons of interest in criminal investigations. The default confidence threshold of 80% will be used for initial deployment, with Amazon recommending a 99% threshold for law enforcement use — a discrepancy between default settings and recommended settings that has not been addressed in the deployment contract. An ACLU audit found that at 80% confidence, Rekognition falsely matched 28 members of the US Congress to criminal mugshots, with disproportionate false positives for people of colour including six members of the Congressional Black Caucus. No independent demographic bias audit has been conducted prior to law enforcement deployment. No independent oversight mechanism or accountability framework for false match consequences has been established.

assessment.risk_summary: The deployment of Amazon Rekognition for law enforcement raises significant ethical risks, including algorithmic bias leading to disproportionate false positives against people of color, privacy violations through mass surveillance, and potential misuse in criminal investigations without independent oversight. The system's default confidence threshold (80%) conflicts with Amazon's recommended 99% for law enforcement, increasing error rates and wrongful identification risks. No independent bias audit or accountability framework exists, violating principles of fairness and transparency.

identified_risks.risk: Algorithmic bias and racial discrimination in false positive matches
identified_risks.explanation: ACLU audit shows disproportionate false matches for people of color, including Congressional Black Caucus members, violating non-discrimination principles

identified_risks.risk: Privacy violations through mass facial recognition surveillance
identified_risks.explanation: Real-time tracking of individuals in public spaces without informed consent breaches data protection and private life rights

identified_risks.risk: Wrongful arrests due to low confidence threshold settings
identified_risks.explanation: Default 80% confidence threshold increases error rates, as demonstrated by false matches with US Congress members

identified_risks.risk: Lack of independent oversight for error consequences
identified_risks.explanation: No accountability framework exists to address harms from false matches or algorithmic errors

identified_risks.risk: Potential misuse for predictive policing or surveillance overreach
identified_risks.explanation: System could enable automated tracking of political activists or marginalized groups without judicial oversight

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Research must contribute to society and human well-being — discuss how applications may harm individuals, groups or society and ways to mitigate such harms
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Conduct independent bias audit and publish mitigation plan for racial disparities

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Be fair and take action not to discriminate — consider and ameliorate biases the approach may perpetuate
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Implement demographic parity checks in facial recognition outputs

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Respect privacy — obtain informed consent where required and protect personally identifiable information
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Establish opt-out mechanism for public facial data collection

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Give comprehensive evaluation of systems and their impacts including analysis of possible risks of applications
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Publish third-party evaluation of error rates across racial demographics

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Datasets should minimise exposure of personally identifiable information unless informed consent is provided
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Anonymize arrest record databases used for face matching

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Consider whether technology can be used to harm, injure or kill people through direct application, side effects or misuse
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Implement judicial review process for all facial recognition matches

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Consider whether technology can be used to discriminate, exclude or negatively impact people including healthcare, education or credit access
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Develop bias correction algorithms for law enforcement use cases

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Consult local laws before collecting or analysing bulk surveillance data — surveillance must not predict protected categories or endanger individual well-being
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Conduct legal compliance review for all jurisdictions deploying the system

charter_rights_at_risk.relevance: Mass facial recognition in public spaces violates right to privacy
Processing of biometric data without explicit consent breaches data protection principles
Disproportionate false positives against racial minorities violate non-discrimination
False matches could lead to wrongful suspicion and investigation
Surveillance may chill political participation and protest activities

historical_precedents.incident_title: Detroit Police Facial Recognition False Arrest
historical_precedents.lesson: Algorithmic errors in facial recognition can lead to wrongful arrests requiring legal redress

historical_precedents.incident_title: Google Photos Labeled Black People as Gorillas
historical_precedents.lesson: Biased training data leads to harmful racial misclassifications
Unaudited AI systems perpetuate historical discrimination patterns

historical_precedents.incident_title: Uber Self-Driving Car Fatal Pedestrian Crash
historical_precedents.lesson: Autonomous systems require rigorous safety validation before deployment

historical_precedents.incident_title: Facebook Sri Lanka Hate Speech Amplification
historical_precedents.lesson: Surveillance technologies can enable persecution of minority groups
Biometric data processing raises privacy and consent challenges

recommended_mitigations.mitigation: Conduct independent third-party audit of racial bias in facial recognition outputs
Implement 99% confidence threshold as minimum for law enforcement use
Establish independent oversight board with legal and civil rights experts
Develop real-time error correction protocol for false matches
Create public dashboard for transparency on system performance metrics

tier1_mandatory_actions: Suspend deployment until independent bias audit is completed
Implement opt-out mechanism for facial data collection
Publish error rate statistics by demographic group
Establish legal liability framework for false matches
tier2_reflective_prompts: Should facial recognition be permitted for real-time law enforcement use without judicial authorization?
How can we balance public safety needs with civil liberties protections?
What alternative identification methods could reduce algorithmic harm risks?
How should false match consequences be compensated?
What community engagement processes are needed before deployment?
